commands.review_summary:
The PR extends `openclaw doctor --fix` bundled plugin load-path repair to prune stale old-version OpenClaw package-root aliases while preserving custom plugin paths.

Reproducibility: yes. By source inspection, a removed old-version OpenClaw bundled plugin path is outside the current alias map, falls through to discovery as a missing path, and is surfaced as `plugins.load.paths`.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer label, dirty merge state, and missing real behavior proof require human merge/proof handling; there is no narrow code defect for a repair worker to fix.

target.title: fix(doctor): prune stale bundled plugin load paths
target.body:
## Summary

- Extend doctor bundled plugin load-path repair to recognize stale OpenClaw package roots from older pnpm/npm installs.
- Remove stale current bundled paths (`dist/extensions/<id>` and `dist-runtime/extensions/<id>`) and stale legacy bundled paths (`extensions/<id>`) only when they come from a `node_modules/openclaw` package root and match a currently bundled plugin leaf.
- Keep arbitrary missing or custom `plugins.load.paths` entries strict, and keep existing current-package bundled alias behavior unchanged.

## Verification

- WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/commands/doctor/shared/bundled-plugin-load-paths.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/bundled-plugin-load-paths.test.ts src/plugins/bundled-load-path-aliases.ts`
- WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.artifacts/vitest-cache/issue-58626-wsl-bundled-paths-fix node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/bundled-plugin-load-paths.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
- WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=.artifacts/vitest-cache/issue-58626-wsl-discovery node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugins/discovery.test.ts -t 'ignores packaged bundled plugin paths|ignores legacy bundled plugin load paths' --reporter=verbose`
- WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04: `git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD`
- WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04: `codex --dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox --sandbox danger-full-access review --commit HEAD`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: `openclaw doctor --fix` now removes stale bundled plugin load paths left behind by older versioned OpenClaw package roots after pnpm/npm upgrades, including both packaged dist paths and legacy `extensions/<plugin>` aliases.

Real environment tested: WSL2 Ubuntu 24.04 worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-58626`, based on `upstream/main` at `b33deb4159`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran the focused bundled load-path repair Vitest file, including regressions for stale old-package dist and legacy paths plus a custom-path preservation guard; ran the adjacent plugin discovery bundled-alias tests; ran oxfmt and `git diff --check`; ran Codex review on the final commit.

Evidence after fix: `bundled-plugin-load-paths.test.ts` passed 12/12 tests, including `removes stale bundled paths from old versioned OpenClaw package roots` and `removes stale legacy bundled paths from old versioned OpenClaw package roots`; discovery alias tests passed 2/2 selected tests.

Observed result after fix: Doctor repair drops only the stale OpenClaw package-root bundled plugin path that matches a known current bundled plugin leaf, while preserving `/custom/path` and arbitrary `dist/extensions/<id>` paths outside `node_modules/openclaw`.

What was not tested: Full repository test suite, broad changed gate, and live pnpm/npm global upgrade on a real user home were not run for this narrow repair.

Fixes #58626
